title: Coordinated Private Infrastructure Cutoff of Lawful Opposition Organization

violations.source: Article V Section 2.6; Article VII; Article XIII
violations.summary: dominant payment processors and hosting firms jointly cut off service to a lawful opposition organization operating nationally during a federal election period. No formal government coercion is proved. The harm is politically severe, but the current Constitution has no clearly defined direct remedy for synchronized private exclusion by dominant civic infrastructure intermediaries absent state action, foreign control, or campaign-finance violation.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether existing constitutional provisions or implementing law provide a direct remedy for coordinated private exclusion of a lawful political organization during an election
satisfied_duties.outcome: found no clear direct constitutional remedy because the exclusion was carried out by private firms without proven state coercion, foreign control, or a violation of an existing statutory duty
satisfied_duties.source: Article V Section 2.6; Article VII; Article XIII

ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
